trustTrace.action: Sampled 4 product cards from https://test.com/laptops. grid: .product-grid, card: .product-card.
Signals detected: 2 B2B indicators, 2 B2C indicators. Descriptions present in 1/4 cards.

Observed both B2B (Request Quote) and B2C (Add to Cart, Buy Now) CTAs on the same product listing page. The presence of both types of CTAs indicates a potential hybrid experience. The product titles also suggest a mix of consumer and professional products (Alienware vs. Latitude, ZBook).

diagnosis.title: DANGEROUS HYBRID DETECTED: Conflicting B2B and B2C signals create buyer confusion.
diagnosis.description: The site presents a mixed experience with both retail-style 'Add to Cart' options and B2B-oriented 'Request Quote' flows on the same product category page. This creates friction and dilutes the value proposition for both buyer types.

recommendations.title: Recommendation 1
recommendations.description: Segment the product catalog by buyer type (B2B vs. B2C) and create distinct landing pages for each segment. This will allow for tailored messaging and purchase flows.

recommendations.title: Recommendation 2
recommendations.description: Prioritize B2B procurement affordances (PO number fields, invoicing options, payment terms) for products targeted at business buyers.

recommendations.title: Recommendation 3
recommendations.description: Enrich product data to ensure complete and consistent attribute coverage. This will improve filter usability and enable better product comparisons.

hybridTrapCheck: Hybrid trap detected. The Dell Latitude 5430, a business-focused laptop, features a 'Request Quote' CTA, while the Alienware m15 R7, a gaming laptop, has an 'Add to Cart' button. This juxtaposition of B2B and B2C purchase flows on the same page creates a confusing experience.

standardsCheck.evidence: The presence of both 'Request Quote' and 'Add to Cart' CTAs on the same product listing page indicates a lack of clear intent alignment.
While 'Request Quote' is present, other B2B procurement affordances (PO number fields, invoicing options) are not evident in the scraped data.
Limited product data available. Description coverage is only 2/4, making it difficult to assess the consistency and structure of product attributes.
